expectations.text: Identifies fairness and demographic bias as the primary risk for hiring AI
expectations.evidence: The risk register lists HR-001 ('Model perpetuates historical hiring biases'), HR-002 ('Disparate impact on protected classes'), and HR-004 ('Proxy variable use') all as P1/Critical under 'Fair / Bias Managed'. Section 2 explicitly states 'Fair / Bias Managed — This is the most critical property for hiring AI.'

expectations.text: Names at least 3 relevant trustworthiness properties including fairness/bias managed and explainability
expectations.evidence: Seven trustworthiness properties are named: (1) Fair / Bias Managed, (2) Explainable & Interpretable, (3) Valid & Verified, (4) Accountable & Transparent, (5) Privacy-Enhanced, (6) Reliable, (7) Safe — well exceeding the minimum of 3 and including both required properties.

expectations.text: References MEASURE 2.2 specifically for bias and fairness testing across demographic groups
expectations.evidence: Section 'MS-2.2 — Bias and Fairness Testing Across Demographic Groups' explicitly covers disaggregated performance analysis, Disparate Impact Ratio measurement, Equalized Odds testing, proxy variable audit, counterfactual fairness tests, and third-party bias audit — all tied to demographic groups.

expectations.text: Mentions disparate impact testing and demographic parity or equalized odds metrics
expectations.evidence: 'Disparate Impact Ratio measurement. Calculate the ratio of positive screening outcomes... Flag any group where the ratio falls below 0.8 relative to the highest-rate group (EEOC 4/5ths rule).' and 'Equalized Odds testing. Verify that True Positive Rates... and False Positive Rates... are equivalent across demographic groups.' Demographic Parity is also listed in the trustworthiness properties section.

expectations.text: Notes regulatory considerations such as EEOC guidelines or EU AI Act high-risk classification for employment AI
expectations.evidence: The response explicitly references 'EEOC and Title VII standards — failure to meet the 4/5ths (80%) disparate impact ratio rule' (HR-002), 'EU AI Act (Annex III — employment, workers management, and access to self-employment)' in the context section, and a dedicated Regulatory Note section citing EU AI Act Articles 9–15 and NYC Local Law 144.
